steps.title: Identify the causal link being tested
steps.content:
The passage establishes Sheffield United wore red, enjoyed home success, then presents research on how red affects people—associations with power/dominance and responses of caution/hesitation (like with stop signs).

The pivot is crucial: “These findings, if applicable in the context of athletic competitions, could suggest that...” This conditional statement signals that opponents must have experienced a specific psychological effect linked to red.

That effect fills the blank.

steps.title: Match the effect to the correct choice
steps.content:
If research on red's psychological impact applies to opponents facing Sheffield United's uniforms, those opponents would have been influenced by the color.

Choice C directly states this: “influenced by the color of Sheffield United's uniforms.”
  • A rules out any influence
  • B shifts to a counterfactual about opponents' own uniforms (not discussed)
  • D claims conscious awareness rather than the subconscious psychological response the passage implies
.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
